diagrams.title: Algorithm Problem-Solving Process
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing the systematic phases of algorithmic problem solving from understanding to testing
elements: Problem Understanding → Algorithm Specification → Algorithm Design → Analysis → Verification → Implementation → Testing
Feedback arrows from Testing back to earlier phases for refinement
diagrams.purpose: Provides visual framework for approaching algorithmic problems systematically, ensuring no critical step is missed in the problem-solving process

diagrams.title: Master Theorem Decision Tree
diagrams.description: Visual guide for identifying which case of Master Theorem applies to a given recurrence T(n)=aT(n/b)+f(n)
elements: Three branches: Case 1 (polynomially smaller), Case 2 (equal), Case 3 (polynomially larger)
Each case leads to specific asymptotic solution
diagrams.purpose: Quick reference for solving common recurrence relations in algorithm analysis, reducing complex mathematical comparison to simple decision steps

diagrams.title: Loop Invariant Visualization for Insertion Sort
diagrams.description: Step-by-step array state showing how the sorted portion grows while maintaining the loop invariant
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the loop invariant concept concretely: the left portion remains sorted after each iteration, proving correctness by showing the invariant holds throughout execution
